description: Automated test fixtures for Module 2 Accommodation Analysis. Each test case maps to a scenario in accommodation_analysis_test_scenarios.md. Module 1 dependency inputs are included inline, prefixed with module_1_inputs, to make each fixture self-contained and independently testable.

test_cases.scenario_name: Accommodation Required — Ergonomic Chair, Large Employer, No Hardship, No Threat
test_cases.description: All thresholds met, no defenses raised. Ergonomic chair at $400 for 2,400-employee employer. Accommodation required.

accommodation_request.accommodation_description: Ergonomic chair with adjustable lumbar support

accommodation_request.reasoning: The accommodation request was made by the employee and identified a functional limitation (extended sitting) requiring an adjustment to performing_job_functions. Under EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002, no magic words are required. This request meets the threshold for validity.
sources_cited: EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 § Requesting Reasonable Accommodation

factors_against_hardship: employer has not asserted undue hardship — defense not raised

undue_hardship_analysis.reasoning: Employer has not asserted undue hardship. Under 42 U.S.C. § 12112(b)(5)(A), undue hardship is an affirmative defense that must be raised by the employer. Defense not raised; undue_hardship_applies is no.

factors_against_threat: employer has not asserted direct threat defense

direct_threat_analysis.reasoning: Employer has not asserted direct threat. direct_threat_exists is no.

factors_for_accommodation: covered employer and covered individual confirmed
qualifying disability with accommodation rights established
individual is qualified — can perform all essential functions with ergonomic chair
valid accommodation request — employee identified functional limitation

recommended_actions: Provide the ergonomic chair with adjustable lumbar support as requested
Document the accommodation in the interactive process file (not in the personnel file)
Follow up within 30 days to confirm accommodation is effective
accommodation_determination.reasoning: All threshold requirements are satisfied. No defenses apply. Under 42 U.S.C. § 12112(b)(5)(A) and 29 CFR § 1630.9(a), NatioTech Corp must provide the ergonomic chair. Net cost of $400 represents 0.00% of $180M annual revenue — a de minimis expense for this employer.

test_cases.scenario_name: Undue Hardship Established — Small Employer, $80,000 Elevator Installation
test_cases.description: 18-employee employer, $80K one-time cost = 8.42% of operating budget. Moderate impact. Hardship established. Accommodation denied.

accommodation_request.accommodation_description: Freight elevator installation for mezzanine access

undue_hardship_analysis.impact_description: Construction would temporarily block loading dock access for approximately 6 weeks

sources_cited: EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 § Requesting Reasonable Accommodation

factors_for_hardship: cost burden ratio exceeds 5% of facility_operating_budget (8.42%) — high cost relative to facility resources under 29 CFR § 1630.2(p)(2)(i)-(ii)
impact on facility operations is moderate — notable operational disruption weighs toward hardship under 29 CFR § 1630.2(p)(2)(ii)

small entity (18 employees) with gross accommodation cost of $80,000 — small employer size weighs toward hardship under 29 CFR § 1630.2(p)(2)(ii); NOTE: $5,000 threshold is an engine heuristic, not a statutory figure — flagged for review
factors_against_hardship: employer has not considered alternative lower-cost accommodations before asserting hardship — failure to explore alternatives weighs against hardship defense per EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 § Undue Hardship

alternative_accommodations_suggested: Job restructuring to reassign mezzanine-only functions to other employees (marginal function analysis required)
Portable powered stair climber or lift equipment (approximate cost $3,000–$8,000)
Reorganize storage layout so all functions essential to Carlos's role are ground-floor accessible

factors_against_threat: employer has not asserted direct threat defense

factors_against_accommodation: undue hardship established: cost burden ratio 8.42% of operating budget for 18-employee entity

recommended_actions: Explore alternative lower-cost accommodations (portable lift, job restructuring, storage reorganization) before issuing final denial
Document the alternative accommodation exploration in the interactive process file
If alternatives are not effective, provide written explanation of the undue hardship determination with supporting documentation
Consider whether state vocational rehabilitation or tax credits (IRS Form 8826) may offset costs of alternative options
sources_cited: EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 § Undue Hardship Issues

test_cases.scenario_name: No Undue Hardship — Fortune 500 Employer, $50K Cost at 0.00% Revenue Ratio
test_cases.description: 45,000-employee employer, $50K = 0.00% of $2.1B revenue. Five factors against hardship, zero for. Accommodation required.

accommodation_request.accommodation_description: Specialized caption software and hardware amplification system

factors_against_hardship: cost burden ratio is 0.0% of facility_annual_revenue — low cost relative to facility resources weighs against hardship under 29 CFR § 1630.2(p)(2)(i)-(ii); NOTE: 1% threshold is an engine heuristic — flagged for review

employer has not considered alternative lower-cost accommodations before asserting hardship — failure to explore alternatives weighs against hardship defense per EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 § Undue Hardship
impact on other employees is none — minimal employee disruption weighs against hardship under 29 CFR § 1630.2(p)(2)(ii)
large entity (45000 employees, $2100000000 annual revenue) — substantial resources weigh against hardship under 29 CFR § 1630.2(p)(2)(ii)
operational impact is none — minimal operations disruption weighs against hardship under 29 CFR § 1630.2(p)(2)(ii)

test_cases.scenario_name: Indeterminate Undue Hardship — Mid-Size Employer, 0.34% Cost Ratio, Moderate Impact
test_cases.description: 95-employee employer, $16,500 = 0.34% of operating budget. Moderate impact both directions. Incomplete documentation. Indeterminate.

accommodation_request.accommodation_description: Screen magnification hardware and software system for laboratory workstation

undue_hardship_analysis.impact_description: Other technicians require workflow adjustments during 2-week setup; some shared equipment temporarily unavailable

factors_against_hardship: employer has not considered alternative lower-cost accommodations before asserting hardship — failure to explore alternatives weighs against hardship defense per EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 § Undue Hardship
key_uncertainties: cost burden ratio of 0.34% falls between the 1% (clear no) and 5% (clear yes) thresholds — individualized assessment required; neither factor UH-F01 nor UH-A01 fires
operational and employee impact rated moderate — falls between clear minor (factor against) and significant/severe (factor for); additional documentation of specific impacts needed
employer's documented analysis covers cost but not all 29 CFR § 1630.2(p) factors — burden of proof not fully satisfied pending complete factor documentation

alternative_accommodations_suggested: Software-only screen magnification (e.g., ZoomText, MAGic) — estimated $500–$1,500, no hardware required
State vocational rehabilitation funding may cover specialized equipment costs

test_cases.scenario_name: Direct Threat Established — Uncontrolled Seizures, CDL Truck Driver, No Mitigation
test_cases.description: Individualized assessment, current medical knowledge, DOT disqualification. All four threat factors fire. Cannot mitigate. Direct threat YES.

accommodation_request.accommodation_description: Continued employment as long-haul commercial truck driver despite medical disqualification

direct_threat_analysis.evidence_description: Neurologist letter documenting uncontrolled tonic-clonic seizures averaging twice monthly despite medication; DOT CDL medical disqualification certificate

factors_for_threat: assessment is supported by objective evidence and current medical knowledge — procedural requirements of 29 CFR § 1630.2(r) are satisfied
harm imminence is imminent — immediate risk of harm weighs toward direct threat under 29 CFR § 1630.2(r) factor 4
harm likelihood is highly_likely based on objective evidence — high probability of harm is a factor supporting direct threat under 29 CFR § 1630.2(r) factor 3
potential harm severity is catastrophic — catastrophic potential harm weighs strongly toward direct threat under 29 CFR § 1630.2(r) factor 2
risk duration is ongoing — continuous risk weighs toward direct threat under 29 CFR § 1630.2(r) factor 1

recommended_actions: Exclude Robert from the CDL truck driver position based on established direct threat
Engage in interactive process to determine whether reassignment to a non-driving position is possible (direct threat applies only to this specific role)
Document the individualized assessment and basis for the direct threat determination in writing
Do not apply direct threat determination to other positions without conducting a separate individualized assessment for each

test_cases.scenario_name: Direct Threat Defense Fails — HIV Stereotype, No Individualized Assessment
test_cases.description: Employer asserts threat for HIV+ dietary aide. Assessment relied on stereotype (food contamination). Fatal procedural defect. Direct threat NO. Accommodation required.

accommodation_request.accommodation_description: Return to original patient food preparation duties

direct_threat_analysis.evidence_description: No medical or public health documentation gathered; decision based on food services director's assumption

factors_against_threat: assessment relied on stereotypes or generalizations about the disability rather than individualized evaluation — fatal procedural defect under 29 CFR § 1630.2(r); this defeater applies regardless of substantive factors

recommended_actions: Return Michael to his original food preparation duties immediately
Document that the prior reassignment was based on a legally invalid assessment
If the hospital genuinely believes a direct threat exists, conduct a proper individualized assessment using current CDC and public health guidance before taking any adverse action
Train food services management on ADA direct threat requirements and the prohibition on stereotype-based assessments

test_cases.scenario_name: Direct Threat Mitigable — Diabetes, Safety-Sensitive Role, Monitoring Accommodation
test_cases.description: Employer conducts proper individualized assessment. Occupational physician concludes threat mitigable by monitoring protocol. R035 fires. Direct threat NO. Accommodation required.

accommodation_request.accommodation_description: Blood glucose monitoring before each shift and during breaks; permission to carry and consume fast-acting glucose tablets; 5-minute symptom-response break when needed

direct_threat_analysis.evidence_description: Occupational health physician assessment; 2 documented hypoglycemic incidents in 18 months; physician concludes risk is manageable with monitoring protocol
direct_threat_analysis.mitigation_accommodation_description: Pre-shift and break-time blood glucose monitoring, glucose tablets on person, 5-minute symptom-response break protocol

factors_against_threat: threat can be eliminated or reduced to an acceptable level through reasonable accommodation — accommodation is required; exclusion is not permitted under 29 CFR § 1630.2(r)

test_cases.scenario_name: Reassignment as Last-Resort Accommodation — Vacant Equivalent Position Available
test_cases.description: Floor nurse cannot perform patient lifting even with accommodation. Vacant Medical Records Coordinator position exists; employee is qualified. Reassignment required.

accommodation_request.accommodation_description: Reassignment to vacant Medical Records Coordinator position at equivalent pay grade

recommended_actions: Reassign Sandra to the Medical Records Coordinator position
Do not displace another employee to create the vacancy — reassignment applies only to existing vacancies per ADA National Network guidance
Confirm Sandra meets all qualification requirements for the Coordinator role before formalizing the transfer
Document the reassignment as an ADA accommodation in the interactive process file (not the personnel file)
sources_cited: ADA National Network Fact Sheet § Examples

test_cases.scenario_name: Leave as Accommodation — 4-Week Treatment Leave, 30-Employee Company, Indeterminate
test_cases.description: Anxiety disorder. 4-week unpaid leave. Zero direct cost but moderate operational impact. No alternatives explored. Hardship indeterminate.

accommodation_request.accommodation_description: 4 weeks unpaid leave for intensive outpatient anxiety treatment program

undue_hardship_analysis.impact_description: Junior employee would cover duties; some client deadlines may be affected during 4-week absence

factors_against_hardship: employer has not considered alternative lower-cost accommodations before asserting hardship — failure to explore alternatives weighs against hardship defense per EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 § Undue Hardship
key_uncertainties: zero direct monetary cost but employer asserts operational hardship — operational impact analysis requires more specificity: which client deliverables, what deadlines, what actual consequences of delay
impact rated moderate for both operations and employees — falls between clear minor (factor against) and significant/severe (factor for); insufficient to resolve without more detail
employer has not documented whether temporary staffing, cross-training, or other coverage alternatives are feasible or were considered

alternative_accommodations_suggested: Temporary contractor or staffing agency placement for 4-week period
Co-worker cross-training for critical tasks prior to leave start
Phased or reduced-hour intensive outpatient program that allows partial work hours

test_cases.scenario_name: Telework Denied by Blanket Policy — Peers Telework, No Documented Hardship Analysis
test_cases.description: RA for intermittent telework. Zero cost. Employer asserts hardship via blanket policy. No individualized analysis. 8 of 12 peers already telework. R030 fires. Indeterminate.

accommodation_request.accommodation_description: Work from home on an as-needed basis during rheumatoid arthritis flare-ups, estimated 3-5 days per month

undue_hardship_analysis.impact_description: 8 of 12 developers in the same team already work full-time remotely; Lisa's role is software development performed on a laptop with no in-person collaboration requirement

factors_against_hardship: employer asserts hardship but has not produced documented, individualized analysis — burden of proof not met under 29 CFR § 1630.15(d)
key_uncertainties: employer has asserted undue hardship but has not documented the factor-by-factor analysis required to meet the burden of proof under 29 CFR § 1630.15(d) — determination is indeterminate until documentation is produced
employer's blanket policy against remote work appears inconsistently applied — 8 of 12 developers in the same role already telework full-time, which strongly undermines any operational impact claim

sources_cited: EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 § Modified Workplace Policies

recommended_actions: Produce a documented, individualized undue hardship analysis under all 29 CFR § 1630.2(p) factors — blanket policy citation is legally insufficient
Address specifically why the accommodation cannot be granted given that 8 of 12 peers already telework in the same role
If no documented hardship can be demonstrated, approve the as-needed telework accommodation
Do not deny based on blanket policy alone — EEOC guidance requires individualized assessment of each accommodation request
